_comment: Per-tool editorial input to scripts/compose-tool.py. The script computes structural audit flags mechanically; this file supplies the topic-specific substantive caveats, the non-leaf evidence_quality judgements, and any leaf-quality overrides (with reason), plus fast-moving re-search instructions (improvement I8). Keeping these here rather than in the script keeps the script generic across tools.

n_concept_reliable.reason: Mechanically two-sided (one cluster each way), but the FOR cluster (nova-defence) argues utility, not measured reliability, so the leaf effectively rests on a single reliability study (Braesco). Flag thin, not well-evidenced.

n_concept: classification validity is itself contested — read the association leaves in that light
n_concept_reliable: rests on a single dedicated reliability study (Braesco 2022)
n_concept_beyond: exposure and diet-quality adjustment both derive from self-reported diet — over- or under-adjustment cannot be excluded
n_assoc: near-entirely observational, self-reported diet (FFQ) throughout — residual confounding and healthy-user bias are live
n_assoc_magnitude: observational — causal interpretation is not licensed
graded evidence tiers reflect statistical strength/consistency, not causation
n_assoc_confound: the pivotal processing-vs-nutrients dispute — carried live in both directions
adjustment can over-control if nutrients lie on the causal pathway of processing
n_assoc_subgroup: subgroup breakdown comes from a single cohort
'no significant association' for a subgroup is not evidence of safety
n_cause: only four qualifying RCTs, small and short — generalisability limited
n_cause_intake: the causal lever (processing vs energy density / eating rate) is contested
n_policy: fast-moving: a US federal UPF definition is due 2026 and may move these findings
n_policy_guidance: authorities split on the same literature — partly a dispute about evidentiary standards and the unresolved definition
n_policy_labels: strongest evidence targets NUTRIENTS (sugar/salt/fat), not processing — transportability to a UPF-specific label is an assumption

audit_notes.n_concept_reliable: Marked thin by override: mechanically two-sided, but the FOR cluster (nova-defence) argues utility rather than measured reliability, so the leaf effectively rests on one reliability study (Braesco 2022, Fleiss' kappa 0.32/0.34). The AGAINST side is the well-evidenced one here.
audit_notes.n_assoc_confound: The pivotal leaf: dietquality-review (64/66 associations survive diet-quality adjustment) and bmj-cohort (association attenuates after the same adjustment) genuinely contradict each other and are typed as an empirical-contradiction crosslink. Retained on both sides rather than resolved.
audit_notes.n_assoc_subgroup: Both directions would come from the single bmj-cohort study, so the SUPPORTS side is single-cluster and no independent AGAINST was located; the umbrella review is carried as COMPLICATES (total-UPF exposure can't confirm or refute concentration). Flagged thin rather than padded.
audit_notes.n_policy_labels: The single FOR cluster (chile-labels) is strong but targets NUTRIENT labels, not the processing category; no study of a processing-specific label exists, so the AGAINST slot is deliberately empty. Scoped to purchases, not consumption or health.
audit_notes.n_cause_intake: Counter-signal preserved: two FOR clusters (hall-rct, dicken-rct) plus an independent AGAINST/COMPLICATES cluster (rct-review, 30/42 outcomes null) — the review arguing against a clear causal link was retained, not pruned.

fast_moving_research.n_policy_guidance: Re-run the FDA/USDA federal 'ultra-processed food' definition search before any republish — a federal definition was due in 2026 and would move this leaf.
fast_moving_research.n_policy_ban_note: no ban leaf in this tool; kept as a reminder that policy leaves here are fast-moving
fast_moving_research.n_cause_intake: Re-check the Nature Medicine critique/reply thread on Dicken 2025 before republish — the dispute was active at authoring time.
